Politics.headline: Avi Lewis vows 'NDP comeback starts now' after winning leadership race
Politics.detail: Avi Lewis has been elected as the new federal NDP leader and has promised a comeback for the party.

Politics.headline: Pakistan maintains 'delicate balancing act' as it hosts Iran talks
Politics.detail: Pakistan is trying to maintain a 'delicate balancing act' while hosting talks with Iran.

Politics.headline: Open: "Face the Nation," March 29, 2026
Politics.detail: This week on "Face the Nation with Margaret Brennan," Border Czar Tom Homan joins as Congress fails to agree on funding for DHS. Plus, as the conflict in Iran intensifies, the top Democrat on the House Intelligence Committee, Rep. Jim Himes, joins.

Politics.headline: The West Block: March 29
Politics.detail: The West Block discusses current political events.

Politics.headline: As prime minister, Mark Carney makes Question Period a low priority
Politics.detail: Mark Carney's approach to Question Period as prime minister is under scrutiny.

Finance & Economy.headline: Financial markets fall to new 2026 low as oil rises again
Finance & Economy.detail: Consumer sentiment in the economy dropped to its lowest level since December, as investors, businesses and consumers worry about the Iran conflict. Rising gas prices are weighing down Americans' confidence in the economy.

Finance & Economy.headline: From oil to food to markets: How a month of war on Iran has remade the world economy
Finance & Economy.detail: One month into the US and Israeli war on Iran, the shock is upending everything from supply chains to air travel. Global oil prices have skyrocketed since the military conflict began in late February, mainly due to the near-closure of the Strait of Hormuz.

Finance & Economy.headline: As Iran war grinds on, rising toll on global economy has many worried about recovery
Finance & Economy.detail: The war with Iran has driven up prices, darkened the outlook for the world economy, sent global stock markets reeling and forced developing countries to ration fuel and subsidize energy costs to protect their poorest. Ongoing strikes and counterstrikes on Persian Gulf refineries, pipelines, gas fields and tanker terminals threaten to prolong the global economic pain for months, even years.

Finance & Economy.headline: Fintech case exposes troubling misapplication of competition law
Finance & Economy.detail: Indonesia's largest digital finance competition case has reached a decisive conclusion, with the Business Competition Supervisory Commission (KPPU) ruling that 97 financial technology peer-to-peer (P2P) lending platforms violated Article 5 of Law No. 5/1999 on Monopolistic Practices and Unfair Business Competition. The case centers on an interest rate ceiling set collectively within AFPI's code of conduct.

Finance & Economy.headline: NAB Consumer Sentiment Survey, Q1 2026: Cost pressures peak as consumers switch
Finance & Economy.detail: Australian households are facing the highest cost of living pressure in more than a decade but are responding by actively switching providers and trading down, rather than sharply cutting spending, according to the latest NAB Consumer Sentiment Survey. NAB's Consumer Stress Index rose to 59.1 in the March quarter, the highest level since 2014 and above its long run average, driven by rising prices for essentials including groceries, utilities, rent and mortgages.

Technology.headline: Microsoft Warns of Hackers Supercharging Cyberattacks With AI
Technology.detail: AI is becoming a force multiplier for cybercriminals, helping threat actors automate phishing, malware development, and reconnaissance while accelerating threat actor tactics.

Technology.headline: Google Primes Chrome for a Post-Quantum World
Technology.detail: Google is testing a new certificate architecture in Chrome designed to protect HTTPS and TLS connections from future quantum computing threats.

Technology.headline: AI Glasses Shipments Grow 322% in 2025
Technology.detail: AI glasses shipments soared 322% in 2025 to 8.7 million units as advances in multimodal AI and lighter wearable designs gain popularity.

Technology.headline: Soon publishers won't stand a chance': literary world in struggle to detect AI-written books
Technology.detail: US release of horror novel Shy Girl cancelled and UK book discontinued after suspected AI use, as publishers feel 'cold shiver'.

Technology.headline: Two in five Australian GPs use AI scribes to record patient notes – but do they trade care for convenience?
Technology.detail: Some doctors argue it allows them to better connect with patients, but advocates warn the AI technology risks the opposite.

Science.headline: Binding to RNA is not enough—changing its shape is what makes a drug work, study reveals
Science.detail: A new study reveals that a drug's ability to change the shape of RNA is crucial for its effectiveness, not just binding to it.

Science.headline: Scientists Solved the Mystery of Missing Ocean Plastic—and the Answer Is Alarming
Science.detail: Scientists have discovered that the ocean's “missing” plastic has broken down into trillions of invisible nanoplastics now spread through water, air, and living organisms.

Science.headline: Cheap Daily Supplement Appears to Boost Brain Functions in Older People
Science.detail: A recent study suggests that a cheap, daily supplement may enhance brain functions in older individuals.

Science.headline: Quadratic gravity theory reshapes quantum view of Big Bang
Science.detail: A new study shows how quadratic gravity theory reshapes quantum view of the Big Bang.

Science.headline: Scientists Figured Out How to Turn Plastic Waste Into a Parkinson's Drug
Science.detail: Scientists have discovered a method to convert plastic waste into a drug that can be used to treat Parkinson's disease.

Health.headline: Ocean's 'missing' plastic found as invisible nanoplastics
Health.detail: Scientists have discovered that the ocean's “missing” plastic hasn't vanished but has broken down into trillions of invisible nanoplastics now spread through water, air, and living organisms.

Health.headline: Brain 'refreshes' after stroke
Health.detail: After a stroke, the brain may do something surprisingly hopeful—it can “refresh” parts of itself, according to researchers analyzing brain scans from over 500 stroke survivors. The damaged side of the brain appears to age faster, but the brain shows an ability to recover.

Health.headline: New cholesterol guideline focuses on personalized prevention
Health.detail: A major new U.S. cholesterol guideline is shifting the focus toward earlier, more personalized prevention of heart disease.

Health.headline: First functional brain atlas shows how communication networks change from infancy to old age
Health.detail: A recently published study showcases the first functional brain atlas illustrating how communication networks evolve from infancy to old age.

Health.headline: STDs fall in adults, syphilis in newborns rises: CDC data
Health.detail: While sexually transmitted disease rates for U.S. adults fell last year, syphilis in newborns continued to rise, according to new data from the Centers for Disease Control and Prevention.

Sports.headline: UConn stuns top-seeded Duke after Braylon Mullins' 3-pointer with 0.4 seconds remaining
Sports.detail: Braylon Mullins sank a desperation 3-pointer with 0.4 seconds left to give UConn an astonishing 73-72 victory over top-seeded Duke, earning the Huskies a spot in the Final Four after they rallied from a 19-point first-half deficit.

Sports.headline: Rohit Breaks Virat's Record As MI End 14-Year Jinx With Win Over KKR
Sports.detail: Rohit Sharma broke Virat Kohli's record as Mumbai Indians secured their first IPL opening game win in 14 years. They chased the target against KKR.

Sports.headline: Chase Elliott beats Denny Hamlin at Martinsville Speedway for 1st NASCAR win of season
Sports.detail: Chase Elliott outdueled Denny Hamlin at Martinsville to secure his first NASCAR win of the season. The race saw Elliott leading the pack and ultimately taking the checkered flag.

Sports.headline: All Whites await as they prepare to face Chile
Sports.detail: New Zealand's All Whites face Chile in their final game before June's World Cup camp. This match is crucial for players to prove themselves and for the team to build confidence before the World Cup.

Sports.headline: ITTF Men’s & Women’s World Cup Macao 2026 draw sets the stage
Sports.detail: The ITTF Men's & Women's World Cup Macao 2026 is set to commence on March 30, 2026, featuring a new playing format and the world's best players. Reigning champions and top-ranked players are geared up for the competition.

Entertainment.headline: Actor James Tolkan of 'Top Gun' and 'Back to the Future' fame dies at 94
Entertainment.detail: James Tolkan, known for his roles as Mr. Strickland in 'Back to the Future' and Stinger in 'Top Gun,' has passed away at the age of 94. Tolkan had 81 acting credits on IMDb and was a well-known character actor.

Entertainment.headline: Tony-Nominated Actor Mary Beth Hurt, Dead at 79
Entertainment.detail: Mary Beth Hurt, a Tony-nominated actress known for her roles in films like 'Young Adult,' 'The Age of Innocence,' and 'The World According to Garp,' has died at the age of 79.

Entertainment.headline: Ricky Gervais' partner Jane Fallon undergoes breast cancer surgery
Entertainment.detail: Jane Fallon, the partner of Ricky Gervais, has undergone breast cancer surgery. She is currently awaiting confirmation of clear margins, which is expected to take eight to ten days.

Entertainment.headline: Michael Jordan reflects on his life after basketball, his move into NASCAR, and the pressure that still comes with his competitive drive and fame
Entertainment.detail: Michael Jordan discusses his post-basketball life, his involvement with NASCAR, and the ongoing pressures of competition and fame.

Entertainment.headline: 'Dhurandhar: The Revenge' mints over Rs 1350 crore in 11 days
Entertainment.detail: Ranveer Singh's 'Dhurandhar: The Revenge' has earned over Rs 1350 crore worldwide in 11 days. The film continues the narrative of Hamza's rise to power and weaves in his past identity.

Crime & Justice.headline: L.A. County sheriff's deputy dies during Baker to Vegas relay race
Crime & Justice.detail: An L.A. County sheriff's deputy died during the annual Baker to Vegas relay race.

Crime & Justice.headline: Marine corporal accused of stealing, reselling weapons and ammo from Camp Pendleton
Crime & Justice.detail: A U.S. Marine formerly stationed at Camp Pendleton is now in federal custody, accused of stealing weapons of war — including a shoulder-fired missile system — with the intent to resell them in Arizona.

Crime & Justice.headline: Slain college student's mother vows 'fight for justice' after illegal immigrant charged in Chicago killing
Crime & Justice.detail: The mother of a slain Loyola student has vowed to 'fight for justice' after an illegal immigrant was charged in her daughter's killing in Chicago.

Crime & Justice.headline: 90 Day Fiance' alum's boyfriend beats Boca Bash attempted murder case he rips as 'ridiculous'
Crime & Justice.detail: A '90 Day Fiance' alum's boyfriend has won his attempted murder case related to Boca Bash.

Crime & Justice.headline: 1 dead after five-car crash on 10 Freeway in L.A.
Crime & Justice.detail: A five-car crash on the 10 freeway left one person dead Sunday morning.

Education.headline: Govt maintains fuel subsidies to cushion impact of rising global oil prices
Education.detail: The Malaysian government is continuing fuel subsidies to help alleviate the impact of increasing global oil prices, demonstrating a commitment to supporting its citizens amidst economic challenges.

Education.headline: Kedah Education Dept to decide on PdPR based on MetMalaysia readings
Education.detail: The Kedah Education Department will determine the implementation of home-based teaching and learning (PdPR) based on readings from MetMalaysia, prioritizing student safety during severe weather conditions.

Education.headline: It is the government's objective to create an education system that provides every child with access to modern technological opportunities — Prime Minister
Education.detail: Prime Minister Dr. Harini Amarasuriya stated the government's commitment to establishing an education system ensuring every child has access to modern technology.

Education.headline: Warm Weather Advisory Issued by the Natural Hazards Early Warning Centre
Education.detail: The Natural Hazards Early Warning Centre issued a warm weather advisory valid for March 30, 2026, indicating that the heat index is expected to increase to a 'Caution level' in several provinces.

Education.headline: Flashpoint March 29, 2026: Experts weigh in on Michigan's education crisis
Education.detail: Experts discuss Michigan's education crisis, focusing on improving reading and math scores, with discussions around AI literacy, comprehensive literacy, and competition within the school system.

Lifestyle.headline: 'Double standards': Instagram removes Erin O'Connor's pregnancy photos again
Lifestyle.detail: Instagram has removed pregnancy photos of model Erin O'Connor, citing 'double standards'. The incident has sparked debate about censorship and body image on social media platforms.

Lifestyle.headline: Make spring cleaning easier with these 13 editor favorites for scrubbing, cleaning and tidying
Lifestyle.detail: Editors share their favorite products for making spring cleaning more efficient and effective, including tools for scrubbing, cleaning, and organizing.

Lifestyle.headline: 'The highs are extremely high – but the lows are extremely low': when working out becomes an addiction
Lifestyle.detail: This article explores the addictive nature of working out, discussing the highs and lows associated with it and the potential negative impacts on mental and physical health.

Lifestyle.headline: Worries about flying seem to be taking off. Here's how to cope with in-flight anxiety.
Lifestyle.detail: With anxieties about flying on the rise, this article provides tips and strategies for managing and coping with in-flight anxiety.

Lifestyle.headline: Electric bikes can be fast and dangerous. Here's how to stay safe.
Lifestyle.detail: As electric bikes become more popular, this article outlines safety precautions and tips for riders to stay safe while using these potentially fast and dangerous vehicles.

Opinion/Editorial.headline: EDITORIAL: Myanmar continues to resist winds of change
Opinion/Editorial.detail: Despite claims of commitment to democracy, Myanmar's leader Gen. Min Aung Hlaing's regime continues its tyrannical rule, frustrating ASEAN's efforts to resolve the conflict and rigging elections to favor the military's proxy party. The international community widely discredits the parliamentary election as a sham, as evidenced by the dissolution of Aung San Suu Kyi's National League for Democracy before the first ballot was cast.

Opinion/Editorial.headline: EDITORIAL — In harm's way
Opinion/Editorial.detail: The editorial discusses the urgency of minimizing the Philippines' dependence on imported crude oil and creating an environment that encourages Filipinos to seek opportunities at home rather than overseas. It highlights the need for investments, implementation of ease of doing business laws, and focus on education and public health to empower Filipinos.

Opinion/Editorial.headline: VP: All bluff, no bloodbath
Opinion/Editorial.detail: This editorial discusses Vice President Sara Duterte's absence from a House justice committee's impeachment hearing, which is seen as a sign of disrespect for democratic processes. It questions her excuse of focusing on the economy and emphasizes that public office is a public trust that requires accountability.

Opinion/Editorial.headline: The mother of forever defeats
Opinion/Editorial.detail: The US's explusion from the Persian Gulf is yet to be understood by Washington, according to this opinion piece. The author argues that the US should recognize the limits of its power and respect the sovereignty of other nations, rather than face the humiliation of military defeat.

Opinion/Editorial.headline: Get Danantara back on track
Opinion/Editorial.detail: This editorial discusses Danantara's commercial investments and involvement in government's economic development agenda, including capital injections into struggling state-owned enterprises. It suggests that many of its moves appear less like disciplined portfolio-building and more like extensions of the government's economic development agenda.

World.headline: Iran accuses US of plotting ground attack
World.detail: The speaker of Iran's parliament has accused the United States of plotting a ground attack, despite publicly pushing for a negotiated deal, adding that Iranian soldiers are prepared for any potential US ground operation. A US warship with around 3,500 military personnel arrived in the Middle East over the weekend, adding to speculation that a ground operation may be imminent.

World.headline: Police fatally shoot Dezi Freeman, ending seven-month manhunt
World.detail: Dezi Freeman, a fugitive who was on the run for seven months after allegedly killing two police officers in Australian alpine region in August, was shot dead by police after refusing to surrender peacefully. The shooting occurred at a property in northeast Victoria, Australia.

World.headline: Climate Change is Stretching Our Days
World.detail: New research indicates that climate change is causing Earth's rotation to slow down, leading to longer days. The Earth's rotation is currently lengthening by 1.33 milliseconds per century, which could affect precise timekeeping and spaceflight navigation.

World.headline: AI deepfakes blur reality in 2026 U.S. midterm campaigns
World.detail: AI deepfakes are impacting the 2026 U.S. midterm campaigns by blurring the reality. This poses a challenge for voters trying to distinguish between authentic information and manipulated content.

World.headline: Israel expands invasion of southern Lebanon
World.detail: Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu announced that Israel will widen its operation in southern Lebanon, as the fighting between Israeli forces and Iran-backed Hezbollah militants intensifies. He has instructed the military to further expand the so-called buffer zone, vowing to change the security situation there.

Zimbabwe.headline: Zanu PF Mashonaland West leadership psyches supporters ahead of CAB3 public hearings this week
Zimbabwe.detail: Zanu PF Mashonaland West leadership is preparing its supporters for the upcoming Constitutional Amendment Bill No. 3 (CAB3) public hearings.

Zimbabwe.headline: ZimParks issues trip advisory on Mana Pools as rains damage bridges, roads
Zimbabwe.detail: Zimbabwe Parks and Wildlife Management Authority (ZimParks) has issued a trip advisory for Mana Pools National Park due to damage to bridges and roads caused by recent rains.

Zimbabwe.headline: Member of Parliament in dock for diverting CDF money to companies linked to himself
Zimbabwe.detail: A Member of Parliament is facing charges for allegedly diverting Constituency Development Fund (CDF) money to companies with links to himself.

Zimbabwe.headline: Law Society of Zimbabwe unveils new identity
Zimbabwe.detail: The Law Society of Zimbabwe (LSZ) has officially launched a rebranding exercise, signaling a shift in both its image and strategic direction.

Zimbabwe.headline: Zimra seizes firearm at Plumtree Border
Zimbabwe.detail: The Zimbabwe Revenue Authority (Zimra) has seized a firearm at Plumtree Border post.
